profile.channel_mismatch_note: HQ Tulsa OK — outside WWT STOLA territory. Channel mismatch flag applied.

signals.signal_title: Williams Companies $3.1B power innovation commitment for AI data center power — Socrates project $1.6B West Texas
signals.signal_description: Williams Companies committed $3.1B to power innovation projects to supply behind-the-meter natural gas power for AI data center campuses. Flagship Socrates project ($1.6B) is located in West Texas. CEO Chad Zamarin driving AI data center power strategy as core growth pillar. This is an energy revenue play, not internal IT AI investment — signals board-level awareness of AI infrastructure demand.

enrichment_notes: FY2024 revenue $10.5B — ~90% fee-based EBITDA (low commodity exposure). 5,829 employees. HQ Tulsa = channel mismatch. New CEO Chad Zamarin July 2025 (former EVP Corporate Strategic Development) = IT budget reset. $3.1B AI data center power commitment is major strategic signal but is energy revenue play, not internal IT AI spend. SAP confirmed. No CIO identified publicly. No MSP, no CoE. ai_maturity_score=9 (below ≥15). WWT opportunity: limited by Tulsa channel mismatch and low internal IT visibility. Best angle is power innovation infrastructure projects.
